message:
fix(doctor): reject absolute run-artifact paths that still contain . or .. components

retain_run_scoped_artifact_path() is the gate that decides whether
a deserialized artifact path is safe to keep in a RunMeta record.
It already canonicalized the run_dir and trimmed the candidate,
and it already rejected relative paths containing CurDir / ParentDir
components. But the traversal check was guarded by
`!path.is_absolute() && path.components().any(...)`, so an absolute
path that still contained `..` segments passed straight through.

This matters because retain_run_scoped_artifact_path is called
before the target file exists — the usual defense (canonicalize
and compare against canonical_run_dir) doesn't help at that stage,
and fs::canonicalize() on a non-existent path returns None (falling
out of the function as "not run-scoped"). So an absolute candidate
like `/tmp/run/missing/../../outside/capture.mp4` would be accepted
into RunMeta, and only rejected on a later access attempt — if at
all, since different callers canonicalize at different times.

Fix:

- crates/doctor_frankentui/src/runmeta.rs: drop the `!is_absolute()`
  short-circuit. Now *any* candidate whose components contain
  CurDir or ParentDir is rejected regardless of whether it's
  absolute or relative. Absolute clean paths (no . or ..) still
  pass and go through the existing canonicalize-then-
  starts_with-run_dir check further down.
- Added regression test
  `retain_run_scoped_artifact_path_rejects_absolute_parent_components_before_creation`
  that constructs an absolute `<run_dir>/missing/../../outside/
  capture.mp4` (deliberately targeting a non-existent leaf so
  canonicalize() would otherwise decline to help), and asserts
  the retainer returns None. The "before_creation" suffix in the
  name is deliberate — it documents the invariant we're
  protecting: safety must not depend on the file existing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
